methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Deep Isolation, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Elizabeth Muller, has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader nuclear energy comp universe of 11 peers (3 public, 8 private), including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power at the private tier.

Only one triangulation method proved applicable. The stage-progression approach uses the $26M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $52M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy subsector; while 8 private peers exist in the sector, they did not cluster sufficiently to produce three or more datapoints meeting narrow filtering criteria. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise.

The single-method triangulation produces a central estimate of $52M, with a range of $44M (low) to $60M (high). This range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression output but is inherently constrained by reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the lack of direct peer benchmarking at Deep Isolation's stage and the adequacy of the 2.0x multiplier assumption for nuclear energy companies specifically. Investors should recognize this valuation is data-limited and subject to material revision once additional comparable transactions or financials emerge.
